channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Doctors on vacation help save 15-year-old shark attack victim
item.description: A group of medical professionals vacationing in Florida rushed to help a teen girl who was attacked by a shark.
item.dc:creator: Haylee Kennedy, KTLA 5

item.title: New York City girl, 13, sexually assaulted at knifepoint during broad daylight in park: police
item.description: A Hispanic man attacked two 13-year-olds in a New York City park in the middle of the afternoon, forcing them both into the woods, where he sexually assaulted the girl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shooting at splash pad in Detroit suburb injures 9, including 2 children. An 8-year-old is critical
item.description: ROCHESTER HILLS, Mich. (AP) — Nine people were injured, including two young children and their mother, after a shooter opened fire at a splash pad in a Detroit suburb where families gathered to escape the summer heat Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Clooney and Roberts help Biden raise $28 million at a fundraiser featuring dire warnings about Trump
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Some of Hollywood's brightest stars headlined a glitzy fundraiser for President Joe Biden on Saturday night, helping raise what his reelection campaign said was $28 million and hoping to energize would-be supporters for a November election that they argued was among the most impor
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Broadway celebrates a packed and varied theater season with the 2024 Tony Awards
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The 28 Broadway shows that earned at least one Tony Award nomination are hoping Sunday is the day the sealed envelopes break in their favor. The three-hour main telecast from New York City's Lincoln Center will air on CBS and stream on Paramount+ starting at 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Chinese premier promises more pandas and urges Australia to put aside differences
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Chinese Premier Li Qiang's visit to Australia on Sunday focused on positive aspects of the bilateral relationship including shared giant pandas and a rebounding wine trade as he promised a new breeding pair of the rare bears and urged both countries to put aside their dif
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Officials rescue 28 riders trapped upside-down at amusement park
item.description: A ride at Oaks Amusement Park in Portland, Oregon, malfunctioned on Friday afternoon, trapping 28 people upside down, but no injuries were reported and all riders have since been evacuated and medically evaluated.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Iacobazzi, KTLA 5

item.title: Which states could have abortion on the ballot in 2024?
item.description: Deadlines are coming up between now and July 5 in five states where advocates are trying to gather enough voters' signatures to put abortion-related questions on ballots in November's elections.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pilgrims commence the final rites of Hajj as Muslims celebrate Eid al-Adha
item.description: MINA, Saudi Arabia (AP) — Masses of pilgrims on Sunday embarked on a symbolic stoning of the devil in Saudi Arabia. The ritual marks the final days of Hajj pilgrimage and the start of the Eid al-Adha celebrations for Muslims around the world.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Katie Ledecky heading to her fourth Olympics, wins 400 freestyle at US swimming trials
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Katie Ledecky is heading to her fourth Olympics, an accomplishment that seemed unimaginable when she was a 15-year-old kid in London. These days, it wouldn't seem like a Summer Games without her. As steady as ever, Ledecky cruised to victory in the 400-meter freestyle at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California woman was harassed by aggressive black bear she named ‘Big B---ard’ before fatal mauling in home
item.description: The California woman who is the first documented case of a fatal black bear mauling in the state was repeatedly harassed by the bear before her death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gretchen Walsh sets a world record in the 100-meter butterfly at the U.S. Olympic trials
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Gretchen Walsh set a world record in the women's 100-meter butterfly Saturday night, posting a time of 55.18 seconds in a semifinal heat at the U.S. Olympic swimming trials.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Woman, who didn't know she was pregnant, gives birth at Golden Corral
item.description: An all-you-can-eat buffet turned into an all-out surprise when one Mississippi woman, who didn't know she was pregnant, gave birth at a Golden Corral.
item.dc:creator: Gary Burton Jr., KTLA 5

item.title: Much of US braces for extreme weather, from southern heat wave to possible snow in the Rockies
item.description: After days of intense flooding in Florida, that state and many others are bracing for an intense heat wave, while the Pacific Northwest will experience unseasonably cold weather and there is a potential for late-season snow in the Rocky Mountains early next week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shooting at splash pad in Detroit suburb injures 9, including 2 children. An 8-year-old is critical
item.description: Authorities in Michigan say nine people have been injured in a shooting at a splash pad in the Detroit suburb of Rochester Hills.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Multiple injured, including 2 children, in Michigan splash pad shooting; suspect dead by suicide: Authorities
item.description: A "random" shooting in Rochester Hills, Michigan, a suburb of Detroit, on Saturday left nine people injured, the Oakland County Sheriff's Office said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Obama-era DACA policy turns 12
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) — The Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals policy turned 12 on Saturday. Known simply as DACA, the Obama-era order protects from deportation immigrants who were brought illegally to the U.S. as children.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, NewsNation

item.title: General Motors, OnStar faces class action lawsuit in data sharing case
item.description: (WJET/WFXP) — General Motors is under fire for their alleged handling of data collection in a class-action lawsuit against General Motors; Onstar; and LexisNexis Risk Solutions.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: USAA members say the bank is doing little to stop hackers
item.description: Thousands of members say they’ve been the victims of theft from their accounts, and that USAA has done little to help.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Why Arkansas is pronounced 'Arkansaw'?
item.description: Saturday marks 188 years since Arkansas was admitted to the Union as the 25th state on June 15, 1836.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Newly released bodycam footage reveals aftermath of Florida alligator attack that left woman dead
item.description: Newly released body camera footage from the Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission shows the scene after a deadly alligator attack last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs returns key to New York City in response to video of him attacking singer Cassie
item.description: Sean “Diddy” Combs has returned his key to New York City after a request from Mayor Eric Adams in response to the release of a video showing the music mogul attacking R&B singer Cassie.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Eyeing Trump presidency, conservatives want to delay funding fight
item.description: Bullish of former President Trump’s chances of winning back the White House in November, some conservatives are pitching a funding stopgap that would extend into next year rather than expire during a lame duck session when President Biden would still be in office.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missouri abortion ban wasn't about lawmakers imposing religious beliefs, judge says
item.description: A Missouri judge has rejected the argument that lawmakers intended to “impose their religious beliefs on everyone” in the state when they passed a restrictive abortion ban.
item.dc:creator: Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Officials rescue 28 riders trapped upside-down at Portland amusement park
item.description: A ride at Oaks Amusement Park in Portland, Oregon, malfunctioned on Friday afternoon, trapping 28 people upside down, but no injuries were reported and all riders have since been evacuated and medically evaluated.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Iacobazzi, KRON4

item.title: A new form of male birth control isn't a pill or a shot: How does it work?
item.description: The availability of a reversible form of male birth control is coming closer to fruition after researchers presented further evidence that this particular method — which suppresses the production of sperm — is safe, relatively easy and, importantly, effective.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Residents and communities preparing for heat wave that will envelop Midwest and Northeast next week
item.description: Things are about to heat up in much of the U.S. with dangerously hot temperatures in the Midwest and Northeast next week, prompting health officials to urge people to make plans now to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Her dying husband worried she'd have money troubles. Then she won the lottery
item.description: FREEPORT, Pa. (AP) — In the weeks before his death, Karen Coffman's husband worried she might have money troubles after he was gone. But two weeks before he died in April of complications from a brain tumor, the Pennsylvania woman bought a scratch-off state lottery ticket that netted her $1 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Officials rescue 28 riders 'stuck upside down' on amusement park ride
item.description: A ride at Oaks Amusement Park in Portland, Oregon, malfunctioned on Friday afternoon, trapping 28 people upside down, but no injuries were reported and all riders have since been evacuated and medically evaluated.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Iacobazzi, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pornhub removes access in another state over age verification law
item.description: Limiting or banning traffic in states is not new for Pornhub, which has blocked traffic in other states like Utah and Texas due to age verification laws.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Massengill, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kentuckians losing access to Pornhub
item.description: Limiting or banning traffic in states is not new for Pornhub, which has blocked traffic in other states like Utah and Texas due to age verification laws.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Massengill, WGN 9

item.title: Whataburger sues North Carolina-based chain over name
item.description: A Texas-based fast food chain that has plans to expand into North Carolina is suing a locally-based restaurant after they say it violated terms of an agreement that allowed them to use similar names.
item.dc:creator: Emily Mikkelsen, WGN 9

item.title: Elon Musk, SpaceX sued over alleged sexual harassment by former employees
item.description: “Elon Musk trumpets SpaceX as the leader to a brave new world of space travel, but runs his company in the dark ages,” said attorney Anne B. Shaver.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, WGN 9

item.title: Are zero-down mortgages making a comeback?
item.description: A major mortgage lender is offering a new 0% down program, but don't expect a zero-down boom, according to one expert.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, The Hill

item.title: Young workers lonely, disengaged and looking for an exit: Report
item.description: As overall employee well-being declined, 1 in 4 young workers reported experiencing “daily loneliness” in 2023, a new Gallup State of the Workplace report found.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, The Hill

item.title: Parents treating children to Disney World acquire nearly $2K in debt, study shows
item.description: According to a Lending Tree study, 45% of parents taking young children to Walt Disney World in Orlando, Florida have taken on Disney-related debt, a 33% increase from 2022, with the average debt being $1,983.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, The Hill

item.title: Stress, depression linked to heart disease in women: Study
item.description: Women under 50 with mental health issues are nearly twice as likely to develop cardiovascular risk factors.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Kernstine, The Hill
